label: 생성
prompt:
GOVERNING: A single decisive news moment — an unbroken column of anonymous citizens under dark umbrellas moving across the rain-flooded stone plaza of Gwanghwamun at night, read instantly as an ordinary crowd caught mid-march, never a posed or staged tableau.
STYLE: reportage press photograph, natural available light, documentary realism, front-page news immediacy, unposed subjects caught mid-action — not a staged studio portrait or magazine editorial.
SCENE: dozens of ordinary people file in a steady procession under beaded black umbrellas through heavy night rain, their backs and shoulders reduced to near-black silhouettes against a haze of distant traffic and building glow, the historic Gwanghwamun gate softening into rain-blurred depth behind them, and all distant signage reading only as soft glowing color held as pure background texture.
CAMERA: shot on a full-frame body with an 85mm lens at eye level, slight telephoto compression stacking the umbrella line into a dense receding column, handheld with faint motion in the falling rain, f/2.8, natural high-ISO grain of a real night news frame.
FOCUS: sharpest on the nearest two or three umbrella silhouettes in the foreground, focus falling off gently into the rain-softened depth of the marching crowd.
LIGHT: cold blue night ambience cut by scattered warm sodium streetlamps and shopfront glow, light raking off wet umbrella domes and the mirror-like flooded granite, long reflections streaking across the plaza, no studio fill.
